rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATSURA ISHIKAWA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition is not verified. (2) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. (3) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (4) Bank statements have not been filed. (5) An account must set state the period covered by the account. [Prob. C. Sect. 1061(a).] (6) An accounting must include at least four columns, showing the date of each transaction, the source or payee, the description of the receipt or disbursement, and the amount. (7) No allowance or rejection of the creditor's claim was filed, as required by Probate Code Section 9250(b). (The petition is rather vague about the creditor's claim.) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YEWOUBDAR BEYENE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no distribution of the the decedent's scarf, books, and computers need be ordered, as: 1) Petitioner reported having made preliminary distribution of the scarf to the specific devisees, although the scarf has never been inventoried and appraised; and 2) According to petitioner, the decedent's books and computers specifically bequeathed have been disclaimed by the respective devisees, and the books and computers have never been inventoried and appraised.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLARENCE I. WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1) Par. 12 of the petition indicates that petitioner requests costs in the amount of $846.50; however, the prayer of the petition does not include this. An amendment to the petition will be needed if still being requested. An amendment still has not been filed. 2) Petitioner alleges that there is over $624K in one bank. Proof of full FDIC insurance must be filed prior to this estate closing. The supplement filed on 10/21/25 is neither signed by the attorney nor verified by the petitioner. 3) Total to be distributed to the trust is alleged to be $592,612.85. Please provide the calculation, beginning with the stated assets on hand ($624,583.33). Petitioner now alleges that the total distributable amount is $593,459.35, which does not take into consideration the $846.50 costs, and there was no indication that petitioner was withdrawing that request.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD F. POHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/2025 in order to be heard by the judge who hears even number case. Appearance will be required on 10/28/2025 before Judge Roeca The following issues to be addressed on 10/28/2025: The supplement indicates that there will be no probate proceedings for the estate of the deceased executor, but it lacks supporting information. It appears necessary to appoint a personal representative for the estate of Charles F. Pohl, as the distribution cannot be made directly to his own heirs/beneficiaries in this estate. Also, the personal representative of Charles F. Pohl's estate should waive the bond accordingly.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEIL W. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplement" filed on 9/30/25 AMENDED the prayer, including a request for a $12,000 reserve, which does not appear to have been in the petition. (2) An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (3) The proposed distribution of $139,858.05 to each heir is not supported by the assets on hand shown on Exhibit D of the petition, which included shares of four stocks. [CRC 7.651(b) and LRSF 14.35 I.6.] If courtesy copies of an AMENDMENT and proof of service of the amendment (showing that notice was served, using the JC form DE-120, at least 15 days before 12/17/25) are not received by the assigned examiner by 12/3/25, the hearing will be continued again. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

(2) Richard Wada is missing from that list of recipients of the incorrect notice of hearing. No courtesy copies were received by the assigned examiner by the 10/13/25 deadline. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PASQUALINA ANGELINA BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
